name: memorised-them-all
description: Convert any attachment to Markdown and digest it into a token-free knowledge-graph memory — locally.
long_description:
Memorised them All turns a folder of documents into durable, **token-free** memory for Claude, entirely on your machine. It converts every attachment (PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, HTML, EPub, images, audio, and more) to Markdown locally using Microsoft's MarkItDown (kept up to date from upstream automatically), Tesseract OCR, on-device Whisper, and a local Ollama vision model. It then runs an original local pipeline — semantic chunking, local embeddings, local-LLM (with a classical fallback) extraction of entities/relations/facts, embedding+fuzzy entity resolution, and community detection — to build a layered knowledge graph: a global synopsis, per-theme summaries, per-document Markdown notes, an exportable memory bundle, and an offline interactive mind map. Every tool returns only compact metadata or a tiny relevant slice, never document contents, so digesting and recalling whole folders costs ~0 Claude context tokens. Everything is free and open-source and runs locally; the Ollama model server starts on demand and stops after 5 minutes idle. Tuned for Apple M-series silicon (performance-core parallelism, GPU Whisper via MLX, unified-memory-aware concurrency).

It also converts legacy Bengali (Bijoy/SutonnyMJ ANSI fonts) to standard Unicode automatically (font-aware, so mixed English+Bengali documents convert cleanly).

Tools: digest, convert, recall, memory_overview, export_memory, list_digestible, forget, memory_status, open_mindmap.

tools.name: digest
tools.description: Convert + digest files/dirs/globs into local graph memory. Returns metadata only (token-free).

tools.name: convert
tools.description: Convert files/dirs/globs to Markdown locally (legacy Bengali/SutonnyMJ→Unicode); writes .md files. Token-free.

tools.name: recall
tools.description: Return a small, citable slice of memory for a query. Never returns whole documents.

tools.name: memory_overview
tools.description: Synopsis, stats and theme list for a project's memory.

tools.name: export_memory
tools.description: Export memory.md, per-document notes, graph.json and the mind map to a folder.

tools.name: list_digestible
tools.description: List convertible files under a directory (paths/sizes only).

tools.name: forget
tools.description: Delete a project's memory (graph, converted Markdown, vectors, mind map). Irreversible.

tools.name: memory_status
tools.description: Local stack health: Ollama, models, Tesseract, MarkItDown version, projects.

tools.name: open_mindmap
tools.description: Path to the offline interactive mind map.

profile.title: Performance profile
profile.description: How big a local AI to use. 'auto' (recommended) sizes it to your computer. 'micro' is safe on a 4GB machine with no graphics card (offline/classical — always works). 'standard' = a 16GB machine, 'large' = 32GB+. Anything below stays blank unless you want to override the profile.

extract_model.title: Extraction model (Ollama)
extract_model.description: Local LLM for entity/relation/fact extraction & summaries. Default qwen3:4b-instruct (2.5GB; newer-gen, non-thinking so clean JSON, 119 languages incl. Bangla — best quality-per-GB on a 16GB machine). More quality if you have RAM: qwen3:8b (5.2GB). Other solid picks: gemma3:4b-it-qat (4GB, QAT≈BF16, 140+ langs), llama3.2:3b (2GB, lightest), qwen2.5:7b (previous default). Newest/experimental: qwen3.5:4b, gemma4:e2b-it-qat (text+vision).

embed_model.title: Embedding model (Ollama)
embed_model.description: Local embedding model for entity resolution and recall. Default qwen3-embedding:0.6b (0.64GB, 1024-d, 100+ languages incl. Bangla — top multilingual retrieval at a tiny footprint). Alternatives: bge-m3 (1.2GB, explicit Bengali + hybrid dense/sparse matching), embeddinggemma:300m (0.62GB, 768-d), nomic-embed-text (English-only, previous default). NOTE: changing this changes the vector dimension — re-digest a project with reset:true after switching (recall falls back to lexical until you do).

vision_model.title: Vision model (Ollama)
vision_model.description: Captions images that OCR can't read. Default qwen3-vl:4b-instruct (3.3GB; 32-language OCR incl. Bangla, reads charts/diagrams/forms). Lighter: qwen3-vl:2b-instruct (1.9GB). Alternatives: gemma3:4b (3.3GB, 140+ langs), granite3.2-vision:2b (2.4GB, document/table OCR), moondream (1.7GB, tiniest but English-only, previous default).

ocr_lang.title: OCR language(s)
ocr_lang.description: Tesseract language codes, e.g. 'eng', 'eng+ben'. Missing packs are dropped gracefully.

whisper_model.title: Whisper model
whisper_model.description: On-device speech-to-text size (tiny/base/small/medium/large-v3-turbo). Default small (good speed/accuracy balance on a 16GB machine); use medium or large-v3-turbo for max accuracy if you have RAM, tiny/base for very low-resource.

idle_seconds.title: Idle shutdown (seconds)
idle_seconds.description: Stop the on-demand Ollama after this many seconds idle.

workers.title: Parallel workers (0 = auto)
workers.description: Conversion workers; 0 auto-sizes to performance cores.
